title: Jeron Joseph: Sacred Ground — Photography Exhibition (ongoing)
location: Anchorage Museum, 625 C St, Anchorage
description: Photographer Jeron Joseph's documentary images honor and showcase the beauty of western coastal Alaska through seasonal changes and plant life in his home community of Kwigillingok.

title: Drag Me to the Movies: Wicked! — Bear Tooth Theatrepub
location: Bear Tooth Theatrepub, 1230 W 27th Ave, Anchorage
description: Local drag artists present a screening of the film Wicked with surprise performances throughout the movie in this ongoing series at Bear Tooth Theatrepub.

title: Side Stories (Last Podcast on the Left Live)
location: Bear Tooth Theatrepub, 1230 W 27th Ave, Anchorage
description: Live show from the creators of Last Podcast on the Left, presenting 'Side Stories' — a deep-dive into true crime news and paranormal happenings, live at Bear Tooth Theatrepub.

title: Teacher Tales — 3rd Annual Storytelling Event
location: Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, 621 W 6th Ave, Anchorage
description: Third annual storytelling event presented by Teacher Tales and the NAACP Anchorage, supported by the Rasmuson Foundation; seven educators share stories from the heart about learning, with local musician performances throughout the evening.

title: Everyday Anchorage: Duke Russell & Ward Wells — Exhibition Opening
location: Anchorage Museum, 625 C St, Anchorage
description: New exhibition exploring daily life in Anchorage through the vivid paintings of Duke Russell and the documentary photographs of Ward Wells, opening May 1 for a year-long run.

title: Michaela Goade: Song of the Seasons — Exhibition Opening
location: Anchorage Museum, 625 C St, Anchorage
description: New exhibition celebrating Alaska's seasons through the images, sketches, and process videos of Tlingit and Norwegian illustrator Michaela Goade, opening May 1.

title: AK Grown: Childhood in Alaska — Exhibition Opening
location: Anchorage Museum, 625 C St, Anchorage
description: Exhibition tracing nearly a century of childhood in Alaska through photographs from the 1930s to the present, showing how the state's youngest residents view their world.

title: Nicole McTrusty Art Exhibition at Kenai Cultural Center
location: Kenai Cultural Center, Kenai
description: Month-long featured artist exhibition showcasing the work of Nicole McTrusty at the Kenai Chamber's Cultural Center, part of their ongoing artist series.

title: 39th Annual Kenai Peninsula Sport, Rec & Trade Show
location: Soldotna Field House, 538 Arena Ave, Soldotna
description: Three-day outdoor sports and recreation trade show featuring 100+ vendors, fishing trip bookings, Alaskan-made products, a youth fish pond, archery, mechanical bull, food court, and giveaways. Opening night Friday includes food, beer, and wine with entry.
sourceName: Kenai Peninsula Sport Rec & Trade Show

title: Yonder Mountain String Band
location: Atwood Concert Hall, Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, 621 W 6th Ave, Anchorage
description: Live performance by Colorado-based progressive bluegrass band Yonder Mountain String Band at Anchorage's premier performing arts venue.
sourceName: CenterTix / Alaska Center for the Performing Arts

title: Anchorage Citywide Spring Cleanup
location: Citywide, Anchorage
description: Month-long community cleanup campaign organized by the Anchorage Chamber of Commerce; residents and businesses pick up post-winter litter across neighborhoods, parks, and trails using free orange bags. Dozens of pop-up organized cleanups happen throughout May.

title: 35th Annual Anchorage Salmon Run (10K / 5K / 2K)
location: Inlet View Elementary School, 815 L St, Anchorage
description: Annual fundraiser 10K, 5K, and 2K fun run starting at Inlet View Elementary and heading out along the Tony Knowles Coastal Trail. Race proceeds support educational programs at the school; a post-race carnival follows.

title: Pink Cheeks Triathlon (5K Run / 10K Bike / 600-Yard Swim)
location: Seward High School, 2100 Swetman St, Seward
description: Seward Parks & Recreation's annual sprint triathlon open to soloists (13+) and relay teams (8+). Course includes high school cross-country trails, city streets, and a pool swim; all skill levels welcome.

title: Anchorage Cheney Lake Park Community Cleanup
location: Cheney Lake Park, Anchorage
description: Community cleanup event at Cheney Lake Park organized by the Anchorage Park Foundation as part of the citywide spring cleanup effort.

title: KPHA Spring Meeting at Pratt Museum
location: Pratt Museum, Homer
description: Kenai Peninsula Historical Association spring meeting at the Pratt Museum in Homer; doors open at 9 AM, meeting at 10 AM, potluck lunch at noon, and an afternoon program of local historical interest at 1 PM.

title: Spring Duathlon Series
location: Anchorage
description: Multi-week spring duathlon series for adults and youth in Anchorage, with supersprint duathlon distances and a virtual participation option.

title: Dance Production — Alaska Center for the Performing Arts
location: Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, 621 W 6th Ave, Anchorage
description: A vibrant dance production celebrating the beauty of creativity found in everyday life, presented at the Alaska Center for the Performing Arts.
sourceName: Alaska Center for the Performing Arts

title: Anchorage Running Club Annual Matson Community Trail Cleanup
location: Campbell Creek Trail / Peanut Farm area, Anchorage
description: Annual volunteer trail cleanup organized by the Anchorage Running Club on the trails behind the Peanut Farm along Campbell Creek.

title: Anchorage Waterways Creek Cleanup
location: Chester Creek and area waterways, Anchorage
description: Annual Anchorage Waterways Council creek cleanup where volunteers in canoes, kayaks, and waders remove trash from local streams and waterways; multiple cleanup dates and locations throughout May.

title: Kinderlauf Family Fun Run (2K / 5K)
location: APU Moseley Sports Center, 4500 Diplomacy Dr, Anchorage
description: 6th annual fun run hosted by Rilke Schule German Immersion School; 2K and 5K routes, costume-friendly, with medals for all finishing kids and a commemorative t-shirt. Proceeds support the school.

title: ATLiens
location: Williwaw Social, 609 F St, Anchorage
description: Live performance by electronic music duo ATLiens at Williwaw Social.

title: Jo Koy — Stand-Up Comedy
location: Alaska Airlines Center, 3550 Providence Dr, Anchorage
description: Stand-up comedy performance by comedian Jo Koy at Alaska Airlines Center. Event is 21+ unless accompanied by a parent or guardian.

title: Arctic Marine Science: Sikuliaq to Shore — Exhibition Opening
location: Anchorage Museum, 625 C St, Anchorage
description: New exhibition opening May 15 where visitors step aboard a recreated section of the research vessel Sikuliaq to discover how science and scientists deepen understanding of the Arctic marine ecosystem.

title: Anchorage Bike to Work Day
location: Citywide trail network, Anchorage
description: Annual community cycling celebration on National Bike to Work Day; participants commute by bike with treat stations along popular routes. Volunteers count riders to track Anchorage's cycling community growth.

title: Chiodos
location: Williwaw Social Outdoors, 609 F St, Anchorage
description: Outdoor concert by post-hardcore band Chiodos at Williwaw Social Outdoors, part of an outdoor summer concert block party on F Street.

title: With Love and a Major Organ — Cyrano's Theatre Company
location: Cyrano's Theatre Building, 3800 Debarr Rd, Anchorage
description: Quirky modern-day fable described as romantic, funny, and wildly original — a unique exploration of love in the age of technology, directed by Gregg Brevoort. Runs May 15–June 7.

title: Matson Ocean Education Center Grand Opening
location: Alaska Wildlife Conservation Center, Mile 79 Seward Hwy, Portage
description: Grand opening of the new 1,000-square-foot Matson Ocean Education Center at AWCC, a classroom-style facility at the water's edge in Portage Valley offering hands-on education about Cook Inlet's marine ecosystem and endangered beluga whales.

title: Law Enforcement Torch Run 5K
location: Anchorage
description: Annual 5K run organized in support of Special Olympics Alaska, part of the national Law Enforcement Torch Run series.

title: Spenard Farmers Market — Opening Saturday
location: Under the Spenard Windmill (Koot's Parking Lot), 2555 Spenard Rd, Anchorage
description: Opening Saturday of the Spenard Farmers Market's 17th season; weekly market runs Saturdays 9 AM–2 PM through September featuring Alaska-grown produce, local food, crafts, and live music under the windmill.

title: Exit Glacier 5K & 10K Race
location: Resurrection Roadhouse, 31752 Herman Leirer Rd, Seward
description: Seward Parks & Recreation's annual 5K and 10K road race starting at the Resurrection Roadhouse near Exit Glacier and following an out-and-back route. Same-day registration available.

title: Anchorage Market & Festival — Opening Weekend
location: Anchorage Market, 225 E St, Anchorage
description: Opening weekend of downtown Anchorage's largest outdoor market; runs Saturdays 10 AM–6 PM and Sundays 11 AM–5 PM through mid-September with up to 300 vendors offering food, crafts, art, and live entertainment.

title: Gold Nugget Triathlon (Women's)
location: Bartlett High School, 1101 Golden Bear Dr, Anchorage
description: Alaska's premier all-women's sprint triathlon with 1,650 in-person participants; dedicated to improving the lives of women and girls through athletics. Mothers and daughters frequently train and compete together.

title: Shoreline Mafia
location: Williwaw Social Outdoors, 609 F St, Anchorage
description: Outdoor concert by West Coast rap group Shoreline Mafia at Williwaw Social Outdoors.

title: Alaska Arbor Day Tree Planting
location: Various parks, Anchorage
description: Volunteer event on Alaska Arbor Day to plant trees in Anchorage community parks; organized by the Anchorage Park Foundation.

title: SOJA — 1st Tap Outdoor Concert
location: Moose's Tooth Pub & Pizzeria, 3300 Old Seward Hwy, Anchorage
description: Grammy Award-winning reggae band SOJA headlines the first outdoor concert of the season at the Moose's Tooth stage, with H3 opening. Ages 21+ event; doors at 6 PM.

title: Trent Waldron Half Marathon & 10K
location: Westchester Lagoon / Margaret Sullivan Park, W 15th Ave, Anchorage
description: Anchorage Running Club's annual half marathon and 10K on a scenic out-and-back course along the Chester Creek and University Trails; strollers allowed at the back. Race honors Marcie Trent and Larry Waldron, killed by a bear while trail running in 1995.

title: 3 Rings Circus
location: Sullivan Arena, 1600 Gambell St, Anchorage
description: Modern American circus featuring aerial ballerinas, motorcycle high-wire acts, acrobats, clowns, and the Valencia Bros. 'Dueling Wheels of Death' across three stages. Multiple shows May 23–24; a portion of proceeds benefits Big Brothers Big Sisters of Alaska.

title: Sawyer Brown & Montgomery Gentry
location: Alaska Airlines Center, 3550 Providence Dr, Anchorage
description: Live country music concert featuring Sawyer Brown and Montgomery Gentry at Alaska Airlines Center.

title: Purity Ring
location: Wendy Williamson Auditorium, UAA, 3211 Providence Dr, Anchorage
description: Live electronic music performance by Canadian dream-pop duo Purity Ring at Wendy Williamson Auditorium.

title: Eagle River Nature Center Public Nature Program
location: Eagle River Nature Center, 32750 Eagle River Rd, Eagle River
description: Weekly guided nature program at the Eagle River Nature Center beginning May 29; programs explore the trails and natural features of the Chugach foothills for all ages.

title: Little Explorers Toddler Hike — Eagle River Nature Center
location: Eagle River Nature Center, 32750 Eagle River Rd, Eagle River
description: Recurring guided nature walk on the Rodak Trail at a toddler's pace, designed for babies and children up to age 6 with accompanying adults; no registration required, check in by 11 AM.

title: Chase Matthew
location: Sullivan Arena Parking Lot, 1600 Gambell St, Anchorage
description: Outdoor country music concert by Chase Matthew at the Sullivan Arena Parking Lot.

title: Twilight 12K & Skinny Mini 6K
location: Skinny Raven Downtown, 800 H St, Anchorage
description: Skinny Raven Sports' signature Friday night running event with live bands along the course; routes traverse Government Hill, Downtown Anchorage, the Coastal Trail, and Westchester Lagoon. Post-race party at the Rose Garden on the Park Strip with Bear Tooth food and Broken Tooth beverages.

title: Golden Girls: The Laughs Continue
location: Atwood Concert Hall, Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, 621 W 6th Ave, Anchorage
description: Touring tribute stage show celebrating the beloved sitcom The Golden Girls with comedy performances at Atwood Concert Hall.

title: Anchorage Tacos and Tequila Fest (21+ Event)
location: Sullivan Arena Parking Lot, 1600 Gambell St, Anchorage
description: Outdoor festival featuring multiple taco vendors, tequila tastings, a frozen margarita station, Lucha Libre wrestling, a Chihuahua beauty pageant, and a concert headlined by Bone Thugs-N-Harmony, Mark McGrath of Sugar Ray, and Sugar Hill Gang. Ages 21+ event.
